items.reality: They're IPython-specific commands; they only work in IPython/Jupyter environments, not in standalone python execution.
items.example: Save a notebook with `%timeit f()` as `script.py` and run `python script.py` → `SyntaxError: invalid syntax`. Magics are an IPython feature, not Python.

items.reality: %pip installs into the kernel's environment, while !pip runs whatever pip is on PATH — which may be a different Python, causing import errors.
items.example: Open a notebook with the `python3.11` kernel; if your shell's `pip` belongs to `python3.9`, `!pip install httpx` puts httpx in 3.9's site-packages — the 3.11 kernel never sees it.

items.reality: %time runs the code once; %timeit runs it many times and reports a statistically stable average, better for fast operations.
items.example: Use `%time client.embeddings.create(...)` (slow network call — one run is fine). Use `%timeit chunk_text(small_doc)` (microsecond op — needs many runs for a stable number).
